findings.evidence: `rg -n "\bchange\b" TauCeti/Algebra/AlgebraicGroup/HopfAlgebra.lean` reports line 88; the target expands to `(φ : A →ₐ[R] B).toLinearMap.comp ...` and `.ofConv` to match `LinearMap.algHom_comp_convMul_distrib`.
findings.fix: Factor an explicit helper lemma for applying an algebra hom to the convolution product in the exact `BialgHom`/`WithConv` shape needed here, then rewrite by that lemma instead of `change`.
findings.issue: `toLinearMap_comp_antipode` uses `change` to coerce the left-inverse goal through `BialgHom.toAlgHom`, `LinearMap.comp`, and `WithConv.ofConv`, so the proof rests on wrapper definitional equality.

findings.evidence: `rg -n "\bchange\b" TauCeti/Algebra/AlgebraicGroup/HopfAlgebra.lean` reports line 102; the changed goal explicitly mentions `(φ : A →ₗc[R] B).toLinearMap` and `toConv ... .ofConv.comp` to fit `LinearMap.convMul_comp_coalgHom_distrib`.
findings.fix: Add an explicit helper lemma for precomposing a convolution product with a `BialgHom`/`CoalgHom` in this target shape, and use that lemma directly.
findings.issue: The right-inverse part repeats the same brittle `change` pattern, this time through the coalgebra-hom coercion and `WithConv.ofConv`.

summary: The antipode-preservation proof still depends on brittle `change` steps through convolution and hom coercion wrappers. The comments explain the current definally-equal shape, but the proof should expose these transports as explicit lemmas instead of relying on representation details.
